source_pr.title: *: support load data for table with generated columns
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #39885

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Dump a table with generated columns, and load it back, it should work!

In the field mapping step, keep the generated column.
And in `parserData2TableData`, ignore the parserData, just set the generated column data to NULL and let `getRow` fill it.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [X] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Statement-count metrics assertions were unstable (e.g., `USE` counts can vary due to background/session behavior).
root_cause_explanation: The DB statement-count test was flaky because it asserted exact deltas on global statement metrics, which can be affected by additional background/session statements (notably `USE`). Such shared/global metrics are sensitive to unrelated activity and can vary across runs. This PR mitigates the instability by adjusting the expected deltas and removing the unstable `Use` statement-count assertion.
fix_pattern: Avoid asserting exact deltas on shared/global metrics that can be affected by unrelated background statements; either reset/isolate the metric source or relax/remove unstable assertions.

evidence: files: executor/importer/import.go, executor/load_data.go, executor/loaddatatest/BUILD.bazel, executor/loaddatatest/load_data_test.go, executor/loadremotetest/one_csv_test.go, go.mod, server/server_test.go, server/tidb_serial_test.go
